meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Build User Onboarding: Solve 'No Project Ideas' for Students
content.seo_description: Stop staring at empty IDEs. Learn to build high-converting user onboarding flows using Antigravity and Claude Code. Perfect for student portfolios.
content.answer_block: To overcome the 'no project ideas' paralysis, build a modular user onboarding system. By focusing on the first-run experience, you solve a critical SaaS problem while creating a high-value portfolio piece. Use Antigravity for UI scaffolding and Claude Code to iterate on user flows, turning a blank screen into a functional product.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the ultimate adversary. You sit down to code, your terminal blinking in a rhythmic taunt, yet the 'perfect' project idea remains elusive. This is not a lack of talent; it is a lack of scope. Every professional app lives or dies by its first five minutes—the onboarding experience. If you can master the art of guiding a user from 'stranger' to 'power user,' you stop being a student and start being a product engineer.

We are going to dismantle the paralysis of choice by focusing on a single, high-impact feature: the interactive onboarding walkthrough. This project is technically rich, visually rewarding, and exactly what hiring managers look for in a junior developer's repository. Let's trade the anxiety of 'what to build' for the thrill of 'how it works.'

steps.title: Define the Onboarding State Machine
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the user states: Welcome, Tutorial, Action, and Completion. This provides the structural skeleton for your onboarding flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React state machine configuration for a 4-step onboarding flow. Include types for 'idle', 'active', and 'completed' states, with transition handlers for each step.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a new file named onboardingMachine.ts and implement the state machine logic defined by the Antigravity schema.'

steps.title: Build the Interactive Overlay
steps.description: Create a high-contrast, non-intrusive modal system that highlights UI elements. This demonstrates your ability to manipulate the DOM and handle z-index layering.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable 'HighlightOverlay' component that accepts a target element ID. It should dim the background and place a spotlight effect on the target using Framer Motion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate HighlightOverlay.tsx into the main dashboard and ensure it animates smoothly between steps.'

steps.title: Implement Progress Tracking
steps.description: Users need to know how far they are. Build a persistent progress bar that saves state to local storage so users can resume if they refresh.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a ProgressTracker hook that syncs the current onboarding step to localStorage. Include a function to reset progress.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Connect the ProgressTracker hook to the onboardingMachine and add a progress bar component to the header.'

steps.title: Add Contextual Tooltips
steps.description: Don't just show a modal; show a tooltip anchored to the UI component the user is learning about. This requires precise coordinate calculation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Tooltip component that uses Popper.js logic to anchor itself to a DOM element ID, supporting dynamic text content and directional arrows.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Update the onboarding flow to trigger Tooltips instead of Modals for the second and third steps.'

steps.title: Finalize and Polish Transitions
steps.description: The difference between a student project and a professional tool is the 'feel.' Add spring-based animations and exit transitions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor all onboarding components to use Framer Motion 'AnimatePresence' for smooth entry and exit transitions. Add a 'confetti' effect on completion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor components for animation and install canvas-confetti to trigger on the final onboarding step.'

pro_tips: Always build onboarding as a separate, mountable component so it can be toggled off for power users.
Use 'feature flagging' to test your onboarding flow with different delay timings to see what converts best.
Never force an onboarding flow; always provide an 'Skip' button to respect the user's intent.
Track the drop-off rate at each step using a simple console log or analytics event to identify friction points.
Accessibility is key: ensure your onboarding flow is fully navigable via the 'Tab' key and 'Esc' to close.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is live, deploy it to Vercel and add a 'Live Demo' link to your GitHub README. Use the metrics you gathered to write a 'Case Study' blog post explaining your design decisions.
content.conversion_block: You now have a production-grade feature that solves a real business problem. Don't stop here—iterate, deploy, and ship. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
